narrative: This week's active-theme highlights span fire ecology, climate impacts, movement, population dynamics, community structure, conservation, methods, biogeography, plant traits, and evolution. In fire-prone southwestern China, Pinus yunnanensis populations show heritable trait readiness for wildfire, while prescribed burning in sub-Mediterranean pine forests left processionary moth resistance largely unchanged, suggesting fuel-reduction burns need not trade off against pest risk. Climate signals were strong: fine-root trait turnover buffered grassland communities against repeated drought, warming's effect on mast-driven oak-mouse food webs depended on its seasonal timing, and cattle competition with wild elk intensified under drought rather than easing it through facilitation. Methodological advances included three-dimensional step-selection functions for swimming and flying animals, a wingbeat-based method for estimating mass gain in flying foragers, and a new R package, ForestForTrees, for inferring forest structure from remote sensing without manual tuning. In conservation, three decades of IUCN Red List reassessment gave sharks, rays, and chimaeras their first long-run extinction-risk trajectory, while a five-year evaluation of expert elicitation in New South Wales's Saving our Species program found predicted management outcomes often diverged from what was observed. On plant traits and evolution, wild large herbivores were shown to boost plant diversity by suppressing dominant species, and a comparison of climate adaptation with evolutionary adaptation offered conservation practitioners lessons on when deliberate adaptation succeeds versus produces maladaptation.

articles.title: Pinus yunnanensis var. pygmaea individuals are more ready to embrace wildfire in more fire‐prone areas
articles.digest: Pinus yunnanensis var. pygmaea populations in more fire-prone parts of southwestern China show greater trait readiness for wildfire, revealing intraspecific variation in serotiny and fire-adaptive traits.

articles.title: Prescribed burning shows minor impacts on black pine needle terpenes and pine processionary moth larval survival
articles.digest: Prescribed burning in sub-Mediterranean pine forests barely altered needle terpene chemistry or pine processionary moth larval survival, suggesting burns can cut wildfire risk without raising pest susceptibility.

articles.title: Pan‐boreal mapping of forest age and implications for conservation
articles.digest: A pan-boreal mapping framework combining multisource remote sensing and disturbance history reconstructs forest age across the boreal biome, informing carbon budget and biodiversity conservation planning.

articles.title: Reproductive maturity drives persistence of an endangered obligate seeder under future fire regimes irrespective of climate change, while targeted reseeding may enhance recovery
Fluvial Export of Carbon and Nutrients from Boreal Catchments Disturbed by Fire and Permafrost Thaw
Diverse plants recruit functional microbes as engines to drive ecosystem multifunctionality in post-fire forests
Engineering fire: the drivers of low fire intensity over gopher tortoise mounds in a Florida sandhill
Mechanisms and scales in modeling forest responses to changing disturbance regimes
Species turnover drives responses of community fine‐root traits to recurrent droughts
articles.digest: Repeated drought experiments in grasslands show species turnover buffers community fine-root trait resistance, meaning trait shifts within species cannot predict whole-community drought responses.

articles.title: Seasonal timing determines how warming affects mast‐driven food webs
articles.digest: Two decades of oak-mouse data show warming's effect on mast-driven food webs depends on when during tree reproduction the warming occurs, resolving conflicting seed and rodent trend results.

articles.title: Drought and growing season phenology over 35 years modulates species interactions among domestic and wild herbivores
articles.digest: A 35-year dataset reveals cattle do not facilitate wild elk as the grazing optimization hypothesis predicts; competition instead drove elk avoidance of cattle, intensifying under drought.

An intuitive method to calculate the utilization distribution of an animal from step‐selection analysis
articles.digest: A new method converts step-selection analysis output into an animal's utilization distribution, offering an intuitive way to estimate space use from existing habitat-selection models.

articles.title: Three‐Dimensional Correlated Random Walks for Animal Movement and Habitat Selection
articles.digest: Step selection functions are extended into three dimensions for animals that swim or fly, letting researchers model habitat selection using easting, northing, and altitude or depth data together.

articles.title: Habitat fragmentation and amount drive within‐species variation in dispersal kernels and limit transferability across landscapes
articles.digest: Habitat fragmentation and total habitat amount jointly shape within-species variation in dispersal distances, limiting how well generic species-specific dispersal kernels transfer across different landscapes.

articles.title: Riding out the storm: Behavioural responses of a large herbivore to high‐Arctic winds
Asymmetric niche partitioning in large omnivores in response to anthropogenic disturbances within subarctic ecosystems

Assessment of large‐scale spatial variation in age‐specific survival and age at first breeding in a long‐lived species
articles.digest: Nationwide data on over 92,000 individuals reveal spatially structured variation in age-dependent survival and age at first breeding, with implications for population dynamics across a species' range.

articles.title: Using wingbeat frequency to estimate mass gain
articles.digest: A new biologging method uses wingbeat frequency to estimate mass gain in flying animals during foraging, offering a way to track foraging success and energy intake in the wild.

articles.title: Plant compositional dynamics explain the destabilizing effect of eutrophication on grassland productivity
articles.digest: Grassland productivity stability under eutrophication depends mainly on persistent species rather than transient ones, showing nutrient enrichment destabilizes ecosystems via persistent-species dynamics, not diversity loss.

Humans acting as ‘super predators' in Central African forests: trophic downgrading and disruption of predator–prey dynamics
articles.digest: Human hunting in Central African forests acts like a super-predator, downgrading trophic structure and disrupting predator-prey dynamics between leopards, golden cats, and their prey.

articles.title: Statistical analyses of ecological multinomial time series to identify environmental drivers and biotic interactions
articles.digest: A new state-space statistical method models multinomial community time series such as pollen or species-count data, better handling relative abundance, observation uncertainty, and uneven sampling.

articles.title: Habitat complexity and prey composition shape an apex predator's habitat use across contrasting landscapes
articles.digest: Joint species distribution modelling of Finnish lynx snow-track data shows habitat complexity and prey composition jointly shape this apex predator's habitat use across contrasting landscapes.

Insights from three decades of IUCN Red List assessments catalyzing shark, ray, and chimaera conservation
articles.digest: Three decades of IUCN Red List reassessment of sharks, rays, and chimaeras yield an unprecedented extinction-risk trajectory back to 1970, underscoring the group's use in tracking global biodiversity loss.

articles.title: Evaluating a structured expert elicitation approach for adaptive conservation management based on lessons from five years in practice
articles.digest: Evaluating five years of NSW's Saving our Species program shows structured expert elicitation predictions for management interventions often diverge from observed outcomes, informing adaptive management practice.

articles.title: Bee community assembly is regulated by functional traits in pristine tropical forest environments
articles.digest: Functional traits regulate bee community assembly across pristine Amazon forest and open habitats, highlighting trait-based drivers of bee beta diversity relevant to conservation and restoration planning.

articles.title: Satellite hindcasts of foliar traits reveal a subtle but consistent relaxation of conservativeness in a biodiverse mountain grassland over the last four decades
articles.digest: Four decades of satellite hindcasts combined with field trait data reveal a subtle long-term relaxation of leaf-trait conservativeness in a biodiverse Brazilian mountain grassland under warming and drying.

articles.title: ForestForTrees: An R package to infer forest structure from remote sensing data
articles.digest: The new R package ForestForTrees infers forest size-abundance structure from remote sensing using a Bayesian scaling framework, removing the need for manual, site-specific statistical tuning.

Human activities and topographical heterogeneity shape Pinus L. species richness patterns in North America

articles.title: Incorporating sampling bias into ENM/SDM permutation tests: new methods and a case study on neotropical ants
articles.digest: New permutation-test methods correct for spatial sampling bias in species distribution and niche models, reducing spurious environmental associations shown in a neotropical ant case study.

articles.title: Nonlinear dynamics of fish beta diversity and the disproportionate influence of non‐native species in cascade‐dammed rivers
articles.digest: Five decades of fish community data from the cascade-dammed upper Yellow River show nonlinear beta-diversity shifts disproportionately driven by non-native species as hydrological disturbance progresses.

Wild large herbivores promote plant diversity and functional redundancy by reducing dominance
articles.digest: Wild large herbivores increase plant species and functional diversity by reducing the dominance of competitive species, clarifying previously inconsistent findings on herbivore effects on plant communities.

articles.title: Taller trees with higher seed output are more likely to invade temperate forests in Patagonia
articles.digest: Taller Pinaceae trees with higher seed output are more likely to invade temperate Patagonian forests, identifying traits that could help anticipate and manage future tree invasions.

articles.title: Woody encroachment reshapes vegetation structure, diversity, and carbon stocks in a Cerrado wetland
articles.digest: Woody encroachment in a Cerrado wetland is reshaping vegetation structure, diversity, and carbon stocks, illustrating how shrub and tree expansion alters wetland ecosystem function.

articles.title: The impact of native vertebrates on enemy release and plant functional traits during community assembly
What climate adaptation can learn from evolutionary adaptation
articles.digest: Comparing climate adaptation to evolutionary adaptation highlights when human-directed adaptation is likely to succeed or produce maladaptive outcomes, drawing lessons from natural selection theory.

articles.title: Eco‐evolutionary shifts in interactions between a globally invasive plant and below‐ground putative pathogens coincide with shifts in plant performance
articles.digest: Invasive Conyza canadensis populations show evolutionary shifts in below-ground pathogen associations between native and non-native ranges, coinciding with better plant defence where pathogens accumulated.

articles.title: SOUPE: An R package to test and optimise the phylogenetic and ecological representativeness of a taxonomic sample
articles.digest: The new R package SOUPE uses Faith's Phylogenetic Diversity index to test and optimise how phylogenetically and ecologically representative a taxonomic sample is for evolutionary studies.

methods_radar.title: ‘SiteTool': a ‘Shiny' application for field site selection and evaluation
methods_radar.note: New R Shiny application, SiteTool, integrates geospatial data to support field site selection and evaluation for ecological studies.

methods_radar.title: Estimation of Forest Soil Organic Carbon Density in Dry Tropical Valleys Based on Multi-source Data and Machine Learning Algorithm
methods_radar.note: Machine learning model estimating forest soil organic carbon density from multi-source data in dry tropical valleys.

methods_radar.title: Long-Term Vegetation Changes Across the Canadian Forest-Tundra Ecotone Using Historic Air Photos and Satellite Time-Series
methods_radar.note: New workflow pairing historic air photos with satellite time-series to reconstruct long-term vegetation change across a forest-tundra ecotone.

methods_radar.title: Socioeconomic traces of land use intensification in Uruguay: census meets remote sensing data
methods_radar.note: Combines census microdata with remote sensing to trace socioeconomic drivers of land-use intensification — a new data-fusion approach.

methods_radar.title: ForestForTrees: An R package to infer forest structure from remote sensing data
methods_radar.note: New R package applying a simplified Bayesian scaling framework to infer forest size-abundance structure from remote sensing without manual tuning.

methods_radar.title: From video to behaviour: An LSTM‐based approach for automated nest behaviour recognition in the wild
methods_radar.note: New deployment-focused LSTM deep-learning framework for automated nest behaviour recognition from field video in the wild.

methods_radar.title: Mothbox and Mothbot: Automated light trap and data processing system for scalable insect monitoring
methods_radar.note: New open-source automated light trap (Mothbox) and machine-learning processing tool (Mothbot) for scalable insect monitoring.

methods_radar.title: In situ aerial bioacoustic monitoring from extremely noisy drone recordings
methods_radar.note: New drone-based acoustic sensing system filters rotor self-noise to enable aerial bioacoustic monitoring of bird vocalisations from UAVs.

methods_radar.title: Comparative species distribution model framework for marine conservation and its application to loggerhead turtles in the Mediterranean
methods_radar.note: Develops and benchmarks a comparative species distribution model framework, tested on loggerhead turtles for conservation planning.

methods_radar.title: Satellite hindcasts of foliar traits reveal a subtle but consistent relaxation of conservativeness in a biodiverse mountain grassland over the last four decades
methods_radar.note: Combines long-term satellite remote sensing with field trait data to hindcast foliar trait trends over four decades.

methods_radar.title: Harnessing the power of machine and deep learning for transferring joint species distribution models considering the structure of biotic interactions
methods_radar.note: Benchmarks deep and machine learning implementations of joint species distribution models against traditional approaches for transferability.

methods_radar.title: Addressing false negatives and positives in eDNA studies
methods_radar.note: Reviews and improves reporting of detection-error rates (false positives/negatives) in eDNA biodiversity studies.

methods_radar.title: Filtration of airborne eDNA outperforms leaf swabs for terrestrial vertebrate detection in a temperate mixed forest with longer filtration times performing the best
methods_radar.note: Benchmarks airborne eDNA filtration times and compares filtration against leaf-swab sampling for detecting terrestrial vertebrates.

methods_radar.title: Changing patterns of colonisation and persistence during the wolf recolonisation of the human‐dominated Italian alpine region
methods_radar.note: Applies dynamic occupancy modelling to a multi-year dataset tracking wolf recolonisation patterns in the Italian Alps.

methods_radar.title: Spectrally Guided Delineation of Structurally Homogeneous Vegetation Patches Using Sentinel-2 Imagery in Heterogeneous Post-Industrial Landscapes
methods_radar.note: Applies Sentinel-2 spectral analysis to delineate vegetation patches in heterogeneous post-industrial landscapes.

methods_radar.title: Warming increases trophic cascade strength in an aquatic food chain
methods_radar.note: Uses experiments and Bayesian dynamical modelling to test how warming strengthens trophic cascade effects.

methods_radar.title: Effective fine‐scale drone monitoring of wild Asian elephants for conflict mitigation and ecological research
methods_radar.note: Applies drone-based monitoring to track wild elephants for conflict mitigation, benchmarking detection under real field conditions.

methods_radar.title: AI foundation models in plant biology
methods_radar.note: Perspective reviewing how AI foundation models are being applied across plant biology, from genomics to crop phenotyping.

methods_radar.title: Environmental temperature shapes heart size evolution in birds
methods_radar.note: Applies Bayesian phylogenetic mixed models across 915 bird species to test Hesse's rule linking cold climates to larger heart size.
